'''Weldii III''' was a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. It was the site of a brutal [[Unidentified mining colony (Weldii III)|mining colony]] led by the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] [[Lenc]]. After the [[Arms dealer/Legends|gunrunner]] [[Praaelon Di]] sold weaponry to both sides in a local [[Miner/Legends|miners']] [[Rebellion on Weldii III|rebellion]], by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] Lenc tasked a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] with bringing Di back to Weldii III. When the [[Attack on Praaelon Di|attempt]] failed, Di led his own armed force in an [[Attack at Mine Shaft 342|attack]] on Lenc and his staff at [[Mine Shaft 342]] on Weldii III and defeated the administrator.

==Description==
An obscure [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|world]], Weldii III occupied the third [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="UB3" /> in the [[Weldii system/Legends|Weldii system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Southern Core]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The surface of the planet featured a [[Forest/Legends|wooded]] area.<ref name="UB3" />

==History==
Weldii III was home to a [[Unidentified mining colony (Weldii III)|mining colony]]. At some point, the local [[Miner/Legends|miners]] staged a [[Rebellion on Weldii III|rebellion]], in which the [[Arms dealer/Legends|gunrunner]] [[Praaelon Di]] sold weapons to both sides of the conflict. [[Administrator/Legends|Administrator]] [[Lenc]], leader of Weldii III's mining colony,<ref name="UB1" /> eventually responded brutally to the insurrection.<ref name="UB3" />

Lenc learned of Di's involvement in the rebellion<ref name="UB1" /> at some point between [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]<ref name="Date 1 BBY">According to {{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/unfinished1|text=Unfinished Business, Part 1: Gunrunner|int=Unfinished Business (scenario)}}, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] utilized [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]] the [[attack on Praaelon Di]] took place. ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' establishes that the Alliance acquired the resources necessary to start the production of [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighters]] a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which translates to [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Therefore, the attack on [[Praaelon Di]] must have occurred no earlier than 1 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|BBY]].</ref> and [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]].<ref name="Date 3.5 ABY">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/unfinished2|text=Unfinished Business, Part 2: Well-Armed and Dangerous|int=Unfinished Business (scenario)}} features [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]] as a participant in the [[attack on Praaelon Di]]. {{WEGCite|story=Bounty's Due|book=Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook}} depicts [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]] being [[Death/Legends|killed]], while ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' multimedia project, which includes the ''Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook'', to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. Therefore, the attack on [[Praaelon Di]] must have occurred no later than 3.5 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]].</ref> In response, the administrator hired a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] to capture Di and bring him back to Weldii III.<ref name="UB1" />

{{RPG}}
[[File:MineShaft342.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Praaelon Di attacked Administrator Lenc at Mine Shaft 342.]]
The pirate [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Attack on Praaelon Di|caught up]] with Di near a remote [[Moon/Legends|moon]]<ref name="UB1" /> but was ultimately defeated by the gunrunner and his allies.<ref name="UB2">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/unfinished2|text=Unfinished Business, Part 2: Well-Armed and Dangerous|int=Unfinished Business (scenario)}}</ref> Subsequently, Di decided to take the conflict to his adversary. The arms dealer traveled to Weldii III with a group of associates, including three [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] pirates, a [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]], and an [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] demolitionist. Di's force was supplemented by an [[LM-432 crab droid/Legends|LM-432 crab droid]] and a pair of [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel troopers]].<ref name="UB3" />

The group sprung its [[Attack at Mine Shaft 342|attack]] on Lenc at the planet's [[Mine Shaft 342]]. At the [[Time/Legends|time]], the administrator was supervising repairs to the mine from a bunker at the top of the shaft, accompanied by his chief miners, [[Technician/Legends|operators]], and [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]]. Aided by disgruntled local miners, Di and his allies eventually defeated Lenc and his underlings.<ref name="UB3" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
The population of Weldii III included miners, as well as the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] administrator Lenc's staff of bodyguards, chief miners, and operators. The latter group included several [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and [[Mustafarian/Legends|Mustafarians]], as well as members of the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]], [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]], and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="UB3" />

==Locations==
At some point, a brutal mining colony was established on Weldii III.<ref name="UB1" /> In addition, Mine Shaft 342 had been excavated beneath a wooded area of the planet. A bunker, outfitted with a [[Blast door/Legends|blast door]], lay atop the mine in a clearing of the forest.<ref name="UB3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Weldii III was first mentioned in "Gunrunner,"<ref name="UB1" /> the opening installment in "[[Unfinished Business (scenario)|Unfinished Business]]," a series of three scenarios for use with the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Miniatures|''Star Wars'' Miniatures]] game. The series was written by [[Sterling Hershey]],<ref name="UB3" /><ref name="UB1" /><ref name="UB2" /> and the first scenario was published on [[Wizards.com]] on [[February 22]], [[2007]].<ref name="UB1" /> The mining world made its first appearance in the third part of the series, titled "Shafted!" and published on [[March 22]], 2007.<ref name="UB3" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Weldii [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Weldii III, in grid square L-13.<ref name="TEA" />

[[File:Boushh-BetweenTheShadows.png|thumb|right|200px|The appearance of Boushh on Weldii III in "Unfinished Business, Part 3: Shafted!" is dependent on the gameplay outcome of the previous scenarios in the series.]]
The narrative structure of the three scenarios of "Unfinished Business" is dependent on the gameplay outcome of each individual scenario.<ref name="UB3" /> If the player representing Praaelon Di and his allies in-game is victorious in "Gunrunner," the second scenario, "Well-Armed and Dangerous," is to be skipped, and the narrative proceeds with "Shafted!"<ref name="UB1" /> In that case, the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]] fights alongside Lenc's staff on Weldii III in "Shafted!"<ref name="UB3" />

In addition, if in "Well-Armed and Dangerous" the player controlling the bounty hunters wins, Di is considered to be captured and brought back to Lenc<ref name="UB2" /> on Weldii III,<ref name="UB1" /> with "Shafted!" to be skipped.<ref name="UB2" /> This article assumes that as many events as possible of "Unfinished Business" play out as described and that Di and his allies are defeated in "Gunrunner," yet triumph in "Well-Armed and Dangerous" and "Shafted!"
